free_text: Single-Particle Neutron Bound States in Iron Isotopes
and in Cobalt Studied with (p,d) Reactions
dE/dx measurement with Si counter. The
beam was monitored with a Faraday cap and current
integrator. A scintillation counter, was set to count
elastically scattered protons from the target at the
fixed angle of 45 degrees, was used as an additional
check on the normalization of the points in the
angular distributions and was used to normalize the
data taken at angles smaller than 15 degrees, for
which Faraday cap had to be removed.
Transformation of the raw data to corrected energy
spectra was accomplished with the aid of the CONDAC
program and an IBM 7090 computer. The energy scale in
the processed data depends on the incident energy,
and on the effective absorber between the target and
the E counter, which is not known precisely. In the
plotted spectra, the beam energy was assumed to be
22.3 MeV and the effective absorber was determined by
setting Q=0 for the elastic scattering and Q=-9.0 MeV
for the ground-state deuteron group from
counter is used, the effective absorber thickness is
33 mg/cm2 (aluminum equivalent) plus one-half of the
While authors speak consistently of scattering and
(p,d) reactions, compiler believes that actually no
corrections were made for other reactions with outgoing
protons or deuterons, respectively, such as (p,np),
data were compiled as proton production, and the
'(p,d)' data were compiled as deuteron production
The solid angle subtended by the counter.
The total number of counts assigned to
imperfectly resolved peak, and the number of counts
lost due to imperfect separation of particle types.
